parts.text:
In /Users/jaronswab/go/src/github.com/jrswab/axe/internal/tool/url_fetch.go, modify the `urlFetchExecute` function to integrate HTML stripping. Also add the `"mime"` import.

First, read the current file to see its exact state.

The changes needed are:

1. **Add `"mime"` to the import block** (between `"io"` and `"net/http"` alphabetically)

2. **In `urlFetchExecute`, after line 150 (`bodyStr := string(body)`) and BEFORE the truncation check**, add Content-Type detection and conditional stripping:

```go
	bodyStr := string(body)

	// Strip HTML if Content-Type is text/html
	mediaType, _, _ := mime.ParseMediaType(resp.Header.Get("Content-Type"))
	if mediaType == "text/html" {
		bodyStr = stripHTML(bodyStr)
	}
```

3. **Change the truncation check** from operating on `len(body)` to `len(bodyStr)`, and from `body[:maxReadBytes]` to `bodyStr[:maxReadBytes]`:

The current code (lines 151-153):
```go
	if len(body) > maxReadBytes {
		bodyStr = string(body[:maxReadBytes]) + "\n... [response truncated, exceeded 10000 characters]"
	}
```

Must become:
```go
	if len(bodyStr) > maxReadBytes {
		bodyStr = bodyStr[:maxReadBytes] + "\n... [response truncated, exceeded 10000 characters]"
	}
```

This ensures the pipeline is: read → strip (if HTML) → truncate.

After making these changes:
1. Run `cd /Users/jaronswab/go/src/github.com/jrswab/axe && go build ./internal/tool/` to verify compilation
2. Run `cd /Users/jaronswab/go/src/github.com/jrswab/axe && go test ./internal/tool/ -run "TestURLFetch_HTML|TestURLFetch_NonHTML|TestURLFetch_PlainText|TestURLFetch_Missing|TestURLFetch_Non2xxHTML|TestURLFetch_Non2xxNonHTML|TestURLFetch_HTMLStripped" -v -count=1` to verify all 9 integration tests pass
3. Run `cd /Users/jaronswab/go/src/github.com/jrswab/axe && go test ./internal/tool/ -v -count=1 2>&1 | tail -40` to verify ALL tests pass (existing 19 + new 18 = 37 total)

Return:
- Compilation result
- Integration test results
- Full test suite results (pass/fail count)
I'll read the file first, then make the required changes.

state.output:
<path>/Users/jaronswab/go/src/github.com/jrswab/axe/internal/tool/url_fetch.go</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>1: package tool
2: 
3: import (
4: 	"context"
5: 	"fmt"
6: 	"io"
7: 	"net/http"
8: 	"net/url"
9: 	"strings"
10: 	"time"
11: 
12: 	"golang.org/x/net/html"
13: 
14: 	"github.com/jrswab/axe/internal/provider"
15: 	"github.com/jrswab/axe/internal/toolname"
16: )
17: 
18: const maxReadBytes = 10000
19: 
20: var urlFetchTimeout = 15 * time.Second
21: 
22: func truncateURL(urlStr string, maxLen int) string {
23: 	if len(urlStr) <= maxLen {
24: 		return urlStr
25: 	}
26: 	return urlStr[:maxLen] + "..."
27: }
28: 
29: func sanitizeURL(urlStr string) string {
30: 	parsedURL, err := url.Parse(urlStr)
31: 	if err != nil {
32: 		return "invalid-url"
33: 	}
34: 
35: 	if parsedURL.Scheme == "" || parsedURL.Host == "" {
36: 		return "invalid-url"
37: 	}
38: 
39: 	path := parsedURL.EscapedPath()
40: 	if path == "" {
41: 		return parsedURL.Scheme + "://" + parsedURL.Host
42: 	}
43: 
44: 	return parsedURL.Scheme + "://" + parsedURL.Host + path
45: }
46: 
47: func urlFetchEntry() ToolEntry {
48: 	return ToolEntry{
49: 		Definition: urlFetchDefinition,
50: 		Execute:    urlFetchExecute,
51: 	}
52: }
53: 
54: func urlFetchDefinition() provider.Tool {
55: 	return provider.Tool{
56: 		Name:        toolname.URLFetch,
57: 		Description: "Fetch content from a URL using HTTP GET and return the response body as text.",
58: 		Parameters: map[string]provider.ToolParameter{
59: 			"url": {
60: 				Type:        "string",
61: 				Description: "The URL to fetch.",
62: 				Required:    true,
63: 			},
64: 		},
65: 	}
66: }
67: 
68: func stripHTML(raw string) string {
69: 	doc, err := html.Parse(strings.NewReader(raw))
70: 	if err != nil {
71: 		return raw
72: 	}
73: 
74: 	var b strings.Builder
75: 	var walk func(*html.Node)
76: 	walk = func(n *html.Node) {
77: 		if n.Type == html.ElementNode && (n.Data == "script" || n.Data == "style") {
78: 			b.WriteByte(' ')
79: 			return
80: 		}
81: 		if n.Type == html.TextNode {
82: 			b.WriteString(n.Data)
83: 		}
84: 		for c := n.FirstChild; c != nil; c = c.NextSibling {
85: 			walk(c)
86: 		}
87: 		if n.Type == html.ElementNode {
88: 			b.WriteByte(' ')
89: 		}
90: 	}
91: 	walk(doc)
92: 
93: 	return strings.Join(strings.Fields(b.String()), " ")
94: }
95: 
96: func urlFetchExecute(ctx context.Context, call provider.ToolCall, ec ExecContext) (result provider.ToolResult) {
97: 	urlStr := call.Arguments["url"]
98: 	statusCode := 0
99: 
100: 	defer func() {
101: 		safeURL := truncateURL(sanitizeURL(urlStr), 120)
102: 		summary := fmt.Sprintf("url %q", safeURL)
103: 		if statusCode != 0 {
104: 			summary = fmt.Sprintf("url %q (HTTP %d)", safeURL, statusCode)
105: 		} else if result.IsError {
106: 			summary = fmt.Sprintf("%s: %s", summary, truncateURL(result.Content, 120))
107: 		}
108: 		toolVerboseLog(ec, toolname.URLFetch, result, summary)
109: 	}()
110: 
111: 	if urlStr == "" {
112: 		return provider.ToolResult{CallID: call.ID, Content: "url is required", IsError: true}
113: 	}
114: 
115: 	parsedURL, err := url.Parse(urlStr)
116: 	if err != nil {
117: 		return provider.ToolResult{CallID: call.ID, Content: err.Error(), IsError: true}
118: 	}
119: 
120: 	if parsedURL.Scheme != "http" && parsedURL.Scheme != "https" {
121: 		return provider.ToolResult{
122: 			CallID:  call.ID,
123: 			Content: fmt.Sprintf("unsupported scheme %q: only http and https are allowed", parsedURL.Scheme),
124: 			IsError: true,
125: 		}
126: 	}
127: 
128: 	reqCtx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, urlFetchTimeout)
129: 	defer cancel()
130: 
131: 	req, err := http.NewRequestWithContext(reqCtx, http.MethodGet, urlStr, nil)
132: 	if err != nil {
133: 		return provider.ToolResult{CallID: call.ID, Content: err.Error(), IsError: true}
134: 	}
135: 
136: 	resp, err := http.DefaultClient.Do(req)
137: 	if err != nil {
138: 		return provider.ToolResult{CallID: call.ID, Content: err.Error(), IsError: true}
139: 	}
140: 	defer func() {
141: 		_ = resp.Body.Close()
142: 	}()
143: 	statusCode = resp.StatusCode
144: 
145: 	body, err := io.ReadAll(io.LimitReader(resp.Body, maxReadBytes+1))
146: 	if err != nil {
147: 		return provider.ToolResult{CallID: call.ID, Content: err.Error(), IsError: true}
148: 	}
149: 
150: 	bodyStr := string(body)
151: 	if len(body) > maxReadBytes {
152: 		bodyStr = string(body[:maxReadBytes]) + "\n... [response truncated, exceeded 10000 characters]"
153: 	}
154: 
155: 	if resp.StatusCode < 200 || resp.StatusCode >= 300 {
156: 		return provider.ToolResult{
157: 			CallID:  call.ID,
158: 			Content: fmt.Sprintf("HTTP %d: %s", resp.StatusCode, bodyStr),
159: 			IsError: true,
160: 		}
161: 	}
162: 
163: 	return provider.ToolResult{CallID: call.ID, Content: bodyStr, IsError: false}
164: }

(End of file - total 164 lines)
</content>

import (

)

const maxReadBytes = 10000

var urlFetchTimeout = 15 * time.Second
